courses.title: Elements of AI
courses.description: A non-technical introduction to what AI is, how it works, and its societal implications. No math or programming required — built for curious non-coders.

courses.title: Prompt Engineering Interactive Tutorial
courses.description: Anthropic's own hands-on guide to writing effective prompts for Claude. Covers chain-of-thought, few-shot examples, and prompt structuring with real exercises.

courses.title: Building with Claude: API Fundamentals
courses.description: Learn to call the Claude API, manage conversations, and handle tool use programmatically. Assumes basic Python or JavaScript knowledge.

courses.title: Introduction to Claude Cowork
courses.description: Learn to use Claude Cowork — Claude working directly on your files, folders, and apps to complete real tasks autonomously. Covers setup, first tasks, plugins, Chrome and Microsoft 365 integration, and safety best practices. Designed for non-technical users who want to automate repetitive knowledge work.
courses.access_note: Course is free; Claude Cowork requires a paid Claude plan (not available on the free tier)

courses.title: Building Effective Agents
courses.description: Deep dive into designing and deploying agentic AI systems using Claude, including tool use, memory patterns, and safety considerations.

courses.title: Practical Deep Learning for Coders
courses.description: Jeremy Howard's top-down deep learning course that gets you training real models in week one. Opinionated, pragmatic, and widely respected in the ML community.

courses.title: ChatGPT Prompt Engineering for Developers
courses.description: Andrew Ng and Isa Fulford's short course on prompting LLMs via API, focused on building real applications. Fast, dense, and practical.

courses.title: LangChain for LLM Application Development
courses.description: Build chains, agents, and memory-augmented LLM apps using LangChain. Assumes Python comfort and basic LLM API knowledge.

courses.title: CS50's Introduction to AI with Python
courses.description: Harvard's rigorous introduction to AI concepts: search, knowledge representation, machine learning, and neural networks — all implemented in Python.
courses.access_note: Free to audit; certificate costs $199 but is not required

courses.title: Machine Learning Crash Course
courses.description: Google's self-paced ML fundamentals course covering linear regression, classification, neural nets, and TensorFlow basics with interactive exercises.

courses.title: Intro to Machine Learning
courses.description: Kaggle's hands-on introduction to decision trees and random forests using real datasets. Practical, fast, and free with interactive notebooks.

courses.title: Intermediate Machine Learning
courses.description: Covers missing values, categorical encoding, pipelines, cross-validation, and gradient boosting — the techniques used in most real Kaggle competitions.

courses.title: AI for Beginners
courses.description: Microsoft's open-source 12-week curriculum covering neural networks, computer vision, NLP, and symbolic AI with Python notebooks throughout.

courses.title: Scientific Computing with Python
courses.description: A thorough free Python course covering syntax, data structures, OOP, and algorithms — everything you need before touching ML libraries.

courses.title: Neural Networks: Zero to Hero
courses.description: Karpathy builds backprop, language models, and a GPT from scratch in pure Python. Slow, rigorous, and deeply instructive — the best thing on the internet if you want to truly understand how LLMs work.

courses.title: Machine Learning Specialization
courses.description: Andrew Ng's updated ML specialization covering supervised learning, unsupervised learning, and reinforcement learning with Python. Industry standard starting point.
courses.access_note: Free to audit but Coursera will repeatedly prompt you to enroll in the paid track — dismiss these, the content is fully accessible without paying

courses.title: Claude's Model Spec (Documentation)
courses.description: Anthropic's public documentation on how Claude is designed to think and behave. Valuable context for anyone building with Claude or studying AI alignment.

courses.title: Finetuning Large Language Models
courses.description: Sharon Zhou walks through when and how to fine-tune LLMs, with hands-on code. Assumes familiarity with Python and basic deep learning concepts.

courses.title: Natural Language Processing
courses.description: Practical NLP with Python: text classification, tokenization, and word vectors. Good bridge between traditional ML and modern LLM techniques.

courses.title: Neural Networks (But What Is a Neural Network?)
courses.description: Grant Sanderson's visually stunning four-part series explaining neural networks and backpropagation from first principles. No code — just the best conceptual explanation that exists.

courses.title: Hugging Face NLP Course
courses.description: Comprehensive hands-on course covering transformers, tokenizers, fine-tuning, and the full Hugging Face ecosystem. The best resource for working with open-weight models.

courses.title: Functions, Tools and Agents with LangChain
courses.description: Learn to build tool-using agents with LangChain's function calling and LCEL patterns. Practical walkthroughs with real Python code.

courses.title: Project: Automate a Weekly Task with Claude
courses.description: Build a custom Claude Project that automates a real task from your work or life — no coding required, just a free Claude account and what you've learned about prompting.

courses.title: Project: Build a Claude-Powered CLI Tool
courses.description: Build a command-line tool that calls the Claude API to solve a real problem you have — a writing assistant, a code reviewer, a data summarizer. Anthropic's docs and SDK have everything you need.

courses.title: Project: Train and Deploy a Classifier
courses.description: Pick a Kaggle dataset, train a simple classifier, and expose it as a web endpoint using FastAPI or Flask. A portfolio-worthy project that proves you can ship.

courses.title: Project: Reproduce a Zero to Hero Result
courses.description: Implement one of Karpathy's models from scratch — micrograd, makemore, or nanoGPT — and write a clear explanation of what you built and what surprised you.
